---
title: "smol-doc-analyzer"
subtitle: "Local insurance document classification, extraction, and memo generation"
---

**smol-doc-analyzer** is a small, locally deployable LLM pipeline for insurance
document intake — classification, field extraction, and adjuster-style memos —
built as a cost-efficient alternative to frontier API models for high-volume
workflows.

This Quarto site gathers the project guides, architecture notes, and interactive
notebooks into one browsable portal.

## Start here

| Page | What you get |
|------|----------------|
| [Quick Start](quick-start.qmd) | Install, secrets, and a minimal first run |
| [Usage](usage.md) | Full command reference for both pipelines, corpus, training, eval, Discord |
| [Architecture](architecture.md) | Dual-pipeline design and repository map |
| [Notebooks](notebooks/index.qmd) | Walkthrough notebooks rendered in the portal |
| [How-to: launch this site](how-to/launch-quarto-site.qmd) | Preview and render with the Quarto CLI |

## Two inference paths

- **DICIE** (`src/docie/`) — paper Fig. 1: process → classify → extract → respond
  (medical bills / salvage claims). See [DICIE Pipeline](docie_pipeline.md).
- **Memo chain** (`src/pipeline/`) — markdown → classify → extract → vision →
  summarize for adjuster-style memos. See [Usage](usage.md).

All training and demo data is synthetic or public; see
[Data Provenance](data_provenance.md).

## Preview this site

```bash
# requires Quarto CLI — run inside docs/ (see how-to for why)
cd docs && quarto preview
# or from repo root:
./scripts/preview_docs_site.sh
```
